proposed.statement: Under \(\textup{ass:uniform-study-positivity}\) and \(\textup{ass:uniform-treatment-positivity}\), \(C_P(\theta)>0\) implies \(\mathcal M(P,\rho,\gamma)=\varnothing\). Indeed, for any \(Q\in\mathcal M(P,\rho,\gamma)\), the weighted observational-mixture identity may be divided by \(1-\pi(X)\ge\varepsilon\). Since \(c_t\in[C_t^-,C_t^+]\), it follows that \(q_{t0}\in[W_t^-,W_t^+]\), while study exchangeability gives \(q_{t0}\in[V_t^-,V_t^+]\). Thus every nonnegative conditional violation is zero almost surely, contradicting \(C_P(\theta)>0\).
proposed.justification: This proved necessity direction turns a positive no-cancellation functional into a certificate of infeasibility.
proposed.gap: It distinguishes an interval-restriction consequence from the unresolved common-extension converse.
proposed.consumer: It certifies incompatibility without relying on the signed proxy.

reason: The proposition explicitly assumes uniform study positivity, and the proof uses it to divide the weighted mixture identity by \(1-\pi(X)\); its dependency list must therefore include \(\textup{ass:uniform-study-positivity}\).

proposed.statement: For \(P^{\mathrm{can}}\) at \(\theta=(1,1)\), all means lie in \([1/4,8]\), \(\pi(x_j)=e_t(x_j,s)=1/2\), and all observed kernels are valid. For arm \(0\), the published un-clipped intervals are \([0,1]\) and \([2,6]\) at \(x_1\), giving \(H_0^{\mathrm{LRVP}}(x_1)=-1\), and \([0,8]\) and \([2,6]\) at \(x_2\), giving \(H_0^{\mathrm{LRVP}}(x_2)=4\). Hence \(G_0^{\mathrm{LRVP}}=(4/5)(-1)+(1/5)4=0\). For arm \(1\), the published intervals are \([0,2]\) and \([1/2,3/2]\) at both strata, so \(H_1^{\mathrm{LRVP}}=G_1^{\mathrm{LRVP}}=1\). The support-clipped arm-zero intervals are \(V_0=[1/4,1]\), \(C_0=[1/4,8]\), and \(W_0=[17/8,6]\) at \(x_1\), and \(V_0=[1/4,8]\), \(C_0=[1/4,8]\), and \(W_0=[17/8,6]\) at \(x_2\). For arm \(1\), they are \(V_1=[1/4,2]\), \(C_1=[1/4,2]\), and \(W_1=[5/8,3/2]\) at both strata. Thus the only conditional violation is \(9/8\) at \(x_1\) for arm \(0\), so \(C_{P^{\mathrm{can}}}(1,1)=9/10\) and \(\mathcal M(P^{\mathrm{can}},1,1)=\varnothing\) by Proposition 1.
proposed.justification: The finite law exhibits cross-stratum cancellation in the practical signed-average proxy.
proposed.gap: Lanners et al. use the signed expectation operationally in Appendix D/E.3; this calculation shows it can accept despite a proved incompatibility certificate.
proposed.consumer: It is a reproducible stress test for Project STAR-style compatibility maps.

reason: Verification that each observed outcome kernel is a valid probability law uses the explicit formula in \(\textup{def:endpoint-kernel}\), so that definition must be a direct dependency.
Rebuild direct reverse dependencies after adding the omitted uniform-study-positivity and endpoint-kernel edges.
